Your coffee machine includes an energy saving function to reduce power consumption when it is not in use.

By default, the machine will automatically switch to Power Off mode after 9 minutes of inactivity. To turn the machine back on, press either the Espresso button or the Lungo button.

<PIC>

If you want to turn the machine off before it switches off automatically, press the Espresso and Lungo buttons at the same time.

<PIC>

You can also change the automatic Power Off setting by following these steps:

1. Make sure the machine is turned off. Then press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds.

<PIC>

2. The Espresso button will blink to show the current energy saving setting.

<PIC>

3. Press the Espresso button to select your preferred setting:
- Press once: automatic Power Off after 9 minutes.
- Press one more time: automatic Power Off after 30 minutes.
- Press one more time: deactivate automatic Power Off.

<PIC>

4. To confirm and exit the energy saving setting mode, press and hold the Lungo button for 3 seconds.

<PIC>

After this, the selected energy saving setting will be saved and the machine can be used normally.,['Manual07_4', 'Manual07_5', 'Manual07_6', 'Manual07_7', 'Manual07_8', 'Manual07_9']

To change the default Energy Saving Mode setting, please follow these steps:

1. Make sure the machine is turned off. Then press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds.
<PIC>

2. The Espresso button will start blinking. The blinking indicates the current Energy Saving Mode setting.
<PIC>

3. Press the Espresso button to cycle through the available settings:
   - Press once: the machine will automatically power off after 9 minutes.
   - Press one more time: the machine will automatically power off after 30 minutes.
   - Press one more time: automatic power off will be deactivated.

4. Once you have selected the desired setting, press and hold the Lungo button for 3 seconds to exit the Energy Saving Mode setting process.

If you mean how to program the water **volume** for your coffee machine, you can save a custom amount for either the **Espresso** or **Lungo** button by following these steps:

1. **Turn on the machine** by pressing either the Espresso or Lungo button, then wait until the lights stop blinking and stay steady. Steady lights mean the machine is ready.
<PIC>

2. **Fill the water tank with fresh drinking water**, then lift the lever, insert a capsule, and close the lever completely.
<PIC>

3. **Place a cup under the coffee outlet** to collect the coffee while you set the desired volume.
<PIC>

4. **Press and hold the button you want to program** — either Espresso or Lungo. Keep holding it while the coffee flows.
<PIC>

5. **Release the button when the cup has reached your desired amount.** The machine will automatically save this new water volume for that button.

6. From now on, when you press that same button, the machine will dispense the programmed amount automatically.

Default factory volumes are:
- **Espresso:** 40 ml / 1.35 oz
- **Lungo:** 110 ml / 3.7 oz

Tip: If you want to adjust both Espresso and Lungo volumes, repeat the same process separately for each button.,['Manual07_24', 'Manual07_25', 'Manual07_26', 'Manual07_27']

To empty the system before a long period of non-use, for frost protection, or before maintenance, please follow these steps:

**Note:** After the emptying process is completed, the machine will be blocked for about **10 minutes** and cannot be used during this time.

1. **Turn the machine off** by pressing the **Espresso** and **Lungo** buttons at the same time.

<PIC>

2. **Remove the water tank**, then **open the lever** fully.

<PIC>

3. Press and hold both the **Espresso** and **Lungo** buttons for **3 seconds** to enter emptying mode.

<PIC>

4. The two LEDs will **blink alternately**, indicating that the emptying mode has started.

<PIC>

5. **Close the lever** to continue the emptying process.

<PIC>

6. The machine will empty the internal water circuit and then **switch off automatically**.

7. Finally, remove, empty, and clean the **used capsule container** and **drip tray**.

After this procedure, please wait around **10 minutes** before trying to use the machine again.,['Manual07_28', 'Manual07_29', 'Manual07_30', 'Manual07_31', 'Manual07_32']

To reset your coffee machine to its factory settings, please follow these steps:

1. Make sure the machine is turned off. Then press and hold the Lungo button for 5 seconds.

<PIC>

2. The LEDs will blink quickly 3 times. This confirms that the machine has been successfully reset to factory settings.

<PIC>

3. After the reset, the LEDs will continue blinking normally while the machine heats up. Once the lights become steady, the machine is ready to use again.

<PIC>

After the reset, the factory default settings will be restored as follows:

- Espresso cup: 40 ml / 1.35 oz
- Lungo cup: 110 ml / 3.7 oz
- Automatic power-off mode: 9 minutes,['Manual07_33', 'Manual07_34', 'Manual07_35']

To keep your coffee maker in good condition and help it last longer, clean it after use with the steps below:

1. Eject the used capsule  
After brewing, lift and close the lever to drop the used capsule into the used capsule container. This helps prevent capsule residue from staying in the brewing area.

2. Empty the used capsule container and drip tray regularly  
Remove any used capsules, coffee residue, and collected liquid from the capsule container and drip tray. Rinse these removable parts and dry them before placing them back.

3. Unplug the machine and let it cool before cleaning  
For safety, always disconnect the machine from the power supply before cleaning. Wait until it has cooled down, especially around the coffee outlet area.

4. Wipe the coffee outlet regularly  
Use a soft damp cloth to gently clean the coffee outlet and the exterior surfaces. This removes coffee splashes and helps prevent buildup that could affect coffee flow.

<PIC>

5. Remove the maintenance parts for easier cleaning  
The maintenance unit can be taken apart into separate pieces, such as the drip grid, drip tray, and capsule container. Cleaning these parts separately makes it easier to remove coffee residue and keep the machine hygienic.

<PIC>

Important cleaning precautions:
- Never immerse the machine, power cord, or any electrical part in water.
- Do not put the machine or its parts in a dishwasher.
- Do not use strong cleaning agents, solvents, steam cleaners, or pressure cleaners.
- Do not use sharp objects, hard brushes, or abrasive cleaning tools.
- For the machine surface, use a damp cloth and, if needed, a mild cleaning agent.

In addition to daily cleaning, descale the machine when needed. Proper descaling helps maintain water flow, brewing pressure, coffee temperature, and overall performance over time.,['Manual07_47', 'Manual07_48']

The swim platform is the stern area of the boat used for standing or sitting while putting on skis or a wakeboard. The platform surface itself does not “open” like a door.

<PIC>

If you are referring to the hatch under the swim platform—the rear platform hatch for the wet storage compartment—open it as follows:

1. Pull the lock handle up.
2. Turn the lock handle clockwise.
3. Lift/open the rear platform hatch to access the wet storage compartment.

<PIC>

To close it:

1. Close the rear platform hatch.
2. Turn the lock handle counterclockwise and confirm the hatch is securely latched.
3. Push the lock handle down so it sits flat.

Important: The engines will not start if the rear platform hatch is not securely closed.

Safety reminder: Stay away from the swim platform area while the engines are running. Exhaust gases from underneath the platform contain carbon monoxide, which is colorless and odorless and can cause serious injury or death if inhaled.,['Manual09_168', 'Manual09_143']

The livewell is located on the starboard side of the boat at the stern. It is used for holding live bait and fish.

To open the livewell, pull the latch and lift the livewell lid.

<PIC>

In the image above: 1 is the livewell lid, and 2 is the latch.

After opening the lid, you will see the livewell compartment inside.

<PIC>

To add water to the livewell:

1. Push the livewell switch to turn on the livewell pump. This will supply water into the livewell.
2. Once there is enough water in the livewell, push the livewell switch again to turn off the pump.
3. If you need to keep the water moving or oxygenated for bait or fish, use the aerator switch to circulate and aerate the water.

<PIC>

In the switch panel image: 1 is the aerator switch, and 2 is the livewell switch.

Tip: If you need light inside the livewell, push the courtesy light switch; it also turns on the livewell light.,['Manual09_153', 'Manual09_154', 'Manual09_155']

The maintenance setting screen is a function on the boat’s multi-function display that shows how many engine operating hours have accumulated since the last maintenance. It displays the maintenance-hour information separately for the port engine and the starboard engine, so you can easily check both engines at a glance.

<PIC>

After maintenance has been completed, you should reset the operating-hour count on this screen so the display can begin tracking the hours until the next maintenance period.

To reset the number of hours of operation:

1. Tap the “Reset” button at the bottom of the maintenance setting screen.

<PIC>

2. A confirmation message will appear. Tap “YES” to confirm and reset the maintenance-hour count.

If you do not want to reset the hours, tap “NO” to return to the maintenance setting screen without making any changes.

<PIC>,['Manual09_83', 'Manual09_84', 'Manual09_85']

The factory reset screen on the boat’s multi-function display shows a confirmation prompt in the center that reads: “Reset all parameters to factory setting?” At the bottom of the screen, there are two options: “Reset” on the left to proceed with restoring the factory settings, and “Back” on the right to return without resetting.

<PIC>,['Manual09_87']

The trip screen is a page on the multi-function display that shows trip-related operating information, such as engine operating hours, fuel consumption, trip distance, water temperature, speed data, and related usage information.

On the trip screen, the main areas are:

1. Menu — displays the trip data items.
2. Scrollbar — used to move through additional menu items.
3. “Reset” button — used to reset resettable trip data.

<PIC>

The menu shows 4 items at a time. If more items are available, use the scrollbar on the right side of the screen to scroll through the full list.

The trip screen can display items such as:

- Water temperature* — ambient water temperature.
- Average fuel consumption — average fuel economy.
- Trip distance — distance traveled since the last reset.
- Fuel used — fuel consumed since the last reset.
- Fuel flow* — current fuel consumption rate.
- Highest speed — highest recorded speed since the last reset.
- Average speed — average speed since the last reset.
- Engine hours* — total engine operating hours since the boat was new.

<PIC>

Items marked with an asterisk (*) cannot be reset.

To reset a single item on the trip screen, touch and hold the item you want to reset for several seconds.

To reset all resettable display items at once, tap the “Reset” button.,['Manual09_69', 'Manual09_70']

To make the boat move forward, use the remote control levers at the helm.

1. First, make sure both remote control levers are in the neutral position and the area around the boat is clear of swimmers, other boats, docks, and obstacles.

2. Move both remote control levers forward together from neutral. As the levers move forward, they shift the boat into the forward range. Continuing to move the levers farther forward opens the throttle, increasing engine speed and producing more jet thrust.

<PIC>

3. At low speed, the boat may first pass through the TDE/forward control range, which helps improve handling response at low engine speed. When the levers are moved farther forward, the shift gates lift fully up. This allows all jet thrust to be directed rearward, which pushes the boat forward.

<PIC>

For normal cruising, move both remote control levers together and adjust them so both engines run at the same engine speed. The levers may not line up perfectly even when the engines are matched, so use engine speed as the reference.

Important safety reminders:
- Always check that the water around you is clear before shifting.
- Apply throttle smoothly; sudden acceleration can surprise passengers.
- Remember that this jet boat needs thrust to steer. If you pull the levers back to idle or neutral while moving, steering response will be greatly reduced.,['Manual09_46', 'Manual09_50']

When you’re driving the boat, steering response comes from two things working together: the steering wheel direction and the amount of throttle you apply. To steer, turn the steering wheel in the same direction you want the boat to go—right to go right, and left to go left.

<PIC>

When you turn the steering wheel, the jet thrust nozzles at the rear of the boat pivot. This changes the direction of the jet thrust, which changes the boat’s direction. The articulating keel also moves with the jet thrust nozzles to help the boat respond to steering input.

<PIC>

A key point while driving: you need throttle to steer effectively. The strength of the jet thrust determines how quickly and sharply the boat turns. More throttle produces more thrust and a sharper turn; less throttle produces less thrust and a wider, more gradual turn. Except at trolling speed, keep the throttle opened above idle when you are trying to turn or avoid something.

Important safety notes:
- Do not pull the remote control levers back to idle when trying to steer away from objects—you need throttle to steer.
- If the engines stop, steering control is lost.
- Boats do not stop like cars, so scan ahead, keep safe distance, and take early action to avoid obstacles.
- Because the boat steers from the stern, the stern swings outward in the opposite direction of the turn. Be careful near docks, swimmers, or other objects.

Before operating, also check the steering system on land:
1. Make sure the steering wheel is not loose. There should be no in-and-out play or excessive rotational free play.
2. Turn the steering wheel fully right and fully left. It should move smoothly and freely through the entire range.

<PIC>

3. Have one person operate the steering wheel while another observes the stern. Confirm that both jet thrust nozzles move with the wheel: they should point to starboard/right when the wheel is turned right, and to port/left when the wheel is turned left.
4. Check that the articulating keel moves in the same manner as the jet thrust nozzles, with no looseness or delay between the steering wheel and the nozzles.

<PIC>

If the steering wheel feels loose, binds, does not turn smoothly, or the jet thrust nozzles/articulating keel do not move correctly, do not operate the boat until the issue is corrected.,['Manual09_52', 'Manual09_53', 'Manual09_206', 'Manual09_207']

At least one fire extinguisher must be carried on board, and it should be full and ready for use before operating the boat. For added safety, carrying two fire extinguishers is recommended.

For this boat, the minimum required extinguisher is at least one 5-B (B-1) type hand-held portable fire extinguisher. In many areas, a USCG-approved extinguisher is required whenever the boat is operated, so please also follow all applicable local and state boating requirements.

**Recommended storage locations:**

1. **Primary fire extinguisher — lockable storage compartment**  
   Mount one fire extinguisher inside the lockable storage compartment. For this location, a chemical-type extinguisher with a capacity of **2 lb or more** is recommended. The image below shows the intended mounting area inside the lockable storage compartment.

<PIC>

2. **Optional second fire extinguisher — battery compartment**  
   If you carry a second fire extinguisher, mount it in the battery compartment. There is a location label outside this compartment to help identify the placement area. Because this extinguisher is located near the engine compartment, it should be a **clean-agent type** extinguisher designed to displace oxygen using an inert gas, such as **CO₂ or FE-36™**, and it should have a capacity of **5 lb or more**. The image below shows the recommended mounting location in the battery compartment.

<PIC>

**Important note:** A fire extinguisher is not included as standard equipment with this boat. If you do not already have one that meets the proper specifications, please contact your local Boat Dealer or a fire extinguisher dealer for a suitable unit. Also check the extinguisher regularly to confirm it is fully charged and in serviceable condition.,['Manual09_211', 'Manual09_212']

To care for the boat’s throttle cable, lubricate the throttle-cable inner wires at the pulley wheel of the APS (Accelerator Position Sensor).

Before starting any maintenance, make sure the engines are turned off unless a procedure specifically requires otherwise. If you are not comfortable servicing the throttle system, we recommend having the work performed by a Boat Dealer or another qualified marine mechanic.

Steps:
1. Turn off the engines and make sure the boat is secure.
2. Locate the APS pulley wheel area in the engine compartment.
3. Apply water-resistant grease to the throttle-cable inner wires where they run at the pulley wheel.
4. After greasing, move the throttle control gently to confirm the cable operates smoothly without sticking or binding.

<PIC>

Use water-resistant grease for this area, as it helps protect the cable from moisture and keeps the moving parts sliding and rotating smoothly. Regular lubrication is especially important if the boat is used in saltwater or stored for long periods.,['Manual09_263']

When you won’t be using the camera for a while, it’s best to remove the battery from the camera and attach the protective cover to the battery. This helps prevent short-circuiting and also helps preserve the battery’s service life. Before you use the camera again, recharge the battery fully.

<PIC>

To recharge the battery, use the method that matches your charger model:

**For CG-580 charger:**
1. Flip out the charger’s built-in prongs in the direction shown.
2. Attach the battery pack securely to the charger.
3. Plug the charger directly into a power outlet.
4. Charging starts automatically; the charge lamp will blink while charging.

<PIC>

**For CB-5L charger:**
1. Attach the battery pack securely to the charger.
2. Connect the power cord to the charger.
3. Insert the plug into a power outlet.
4. Charging starts automatically, and the red lamp will begin blinking.

<PIC>

Approximate charging time for a completely exhausted battery is:
- **BP-511A / BP-514:** about **100 minutes**
- **BP-511 / BP-512:** about **90 minutes**

When the red lamp stays lit, the battery has reached about 90% or more. For a full charge, continue charging for about **one additional hour** after the red lamp lights steadily. After charging is complete, remove the battery from the charger and unplug the charger or power cord from the outlet.

Please note:
- Only recharge **BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512** battery packs.
- Charge the battery only within **0°C–40°C / 32°F–104°F**.
- If you notice excessive heat, smoke, fumes, deformation, or leakage during charging, unplug the charger immediately and stop using the battery.
- Do not leave the battery in the camera for long periods when the camera is not being used, as a small current may continue to discharge and affect battery life.,['Manual10_10', 'Manual10_12', 'Manual10_13']

If the current battery is exhausted or the camera is powered off, you can replace/install the camera battery as follows. Please use a fully charged compatible battery pack, such as BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512.

1. Turn the camera off first
Set the camera power switch to OFF before opening the battery compartment. This helps prevent power interruption issues while handling the battery.

2. Open the battery compartment cover
Slide the battery compartment lever in the direction shown, then open the cover.
<PIC>

3. Insert the charged battery
Hold the battery with the electrical contacts facing downward, then slide it into the compartment. Push it in until it locks securely into place.
<PIC>

4. Close the battery compartment cover
Press the cover closed until it clicks/snaps shut. The camera may not power on if the cover is not fully closed.
<PIC>

5. Turn the camera on and check operation
After the battery is installed, set the power switch to ON. If the camera still does not operate, please check the following:
- The battery is fully charged.
- The battery is inserted in the correct direction.
- The battery compartment cover is closed tightly.
- The battery contacts are clean and not blocked.

Important: Do not insert the battery in the wrong direction, do not use damaged or modified batteries, and avoid touching the battery contacts with metal objects.,['Manual10_16', 'Camera_09', 'Camera_10']

To power the camera from a household power socket, use the optional AC Adapter Kit ACK-E2. This kit includes the AC adapter, DC Coupler, and power cord, and lets you use the camera without worrying about battery level.

1. Connect the DC Coupler to the AC adapter.
Insert the DC Coupler’s plug firmly into the socket on the AC adapter, as shown below.
<PIC>

2. Connect the power cord to the AC adapter and wall outlet.
Attach the power cord to the AC adapter first, then plug the other end into a household power outlet. Make sure the plug is inserted all the way into the outlet.
<PIC>

3. Route the cord through the groove.
Carefully place the DC Coupler cord into the designated groove. Do not force, pinch, bend sharply, or damage the cord while positioning it.
<PIC>

4. Insert the DC Coupler into the camera.
Open the battery compartment cover, then open the DC Coupler cord notch cover. Insert the DC Coupler into the battery compartment until it locks into position. Route the cord through the notch, then close the battery compartment cover securely.
<PIC>

Important safety notes:
- The AC Adapter Kit ACK-E2 is optional and is compatible with AC 100–240 V.
- Turn the camera off before connecting or disconnecting the power cord.
- Do not handle the power plug with wet hands.
- When unplugging, hold and pull the plug itself, not the cord.
- Keep cords away from heat sources and avoid placing heavy objects on them.
- Do not use the cord if the insulation is damaged.
- After use, unplug the power plug from the household outlet.,['Camera_14', 'Camera_15', 'Manual10_19', 'Manual10_20']

To mount the lens before taking photos, follow these steps carefully:

1. Remove the caps

Remove the rear lens cap from the lens and the body cap from the camera by turning each one in the direction indicated. Try to keep the camera mount facing downward or covered as much as possible to reduce the chance of dust entering the camera.

<PIC>

2. Align and attach the lens

Hold the lens straight in front of the camera mount, then align the correct mounting mark:

- For an EF-S lens: align the lens with the camera’s white EF-S lens mount index.
- For an EF lens or other non-EF-S lens: align the lens with the red EF lens mount index.

After aligning the marks, turn the lens in the direction shown until it clicks into place. The click means the lens is securely locked on the camera body.

<PIC>

3. Set the focus mode and remove the front cap

On the lens, set the focus mode switch to <AF> if you want to use autofocus. If the switch is set to <MF>, autofocus will not operate. After that, remove the front lens cap by pinching the cap tabs and pulling it forward.

<PIC>

Note: When attaching or removing a lens, be careful not to let dust enter the camera through the lens mount. Also avoid touching the lens contacts or the camera’s internal parts.,['Manual10_21', 'Manual10_22', 'Manual10_23']

To install the CF card into the camera before taking photos, please follow these steps:

1. Open the CF card slot cover  
Slide the card slot cover in the direction indicated by the arrow, then swing it open.

<PIC>

2. Insert the CF card correctly  
Hold the CF card with the label side facing toward you. Insert the end with the small holes into the camera, following the direction shown. Push it in gently and straight until it is seated properly. When the card is inserted correctly, the CF card eject button will pop out.

<PIC>

Important: Do not force the CF card in. If it is inserted in the wrong direction, it may damage the card slot or the camera.

3. Close and lock the cover  
Close the CF card slot cover, then slide it in the indicated direction until it snaps securely shut.

<PIC>

4. Confirm the card is ready  
After the cover is closed, check the LCD panel. When the number of remaining shots is displayed, the CF card has been recognized and the camera is ready for photography.

<PIC>

Before shooting, always make sure a CF card is installed in the camera and that the card slot cover is fully closed.,['Manual10_25', 'Manual10_26', 'Manual10_27', 'Manual10_28']

I’m sorry, but I can’t provide instructions for removing the camera’s shutter button. The shutter button is part of the camera’s internal control and sealing structure, and attempting to remove it yourself may damage the camera, affect weather/dust protection, or cause the shutter/AF functions to stop working properly.

If the shutter button is stuck, loose, or not responding, we recommend having it inspected by an authorized camera service technician rather than trying to detach it at home.

For normal use, the shutter button has two stages:

1. Press halfway — this activates autofocus (AF) and automatic exposure (AE), setting the shutter speed and aperture.
<PIC>

2. Press completely — this releases the shutter and takes the picture.
<PIC>

To avoid camera shake or accidental damage, place your fingertip gently on the shutter button, hold the camera firmly with your right hand, and press smoothly rather than forcefully.,['Manual10_31', 'Manual10_32']

The camera uses a small date/time back-up battery to keep the date and time settings. Its typical service life is about 5 years. If the camera’s date/time is reset after changing the main battery, replace this back-up battery with a new CR2016 lithium battery.

Please note: after replacing the date/time battery, the camera’s date/time setting will be reset, so you’ll need to set the correct date and time again afterward.

1. Turn the camera power switch to <OFF>, then open the battery compartment cover and remove the main battery.
<PIC>

2. Pull out the date/time battery holder from the battery compartment.
<PIC>

3. Replace the battery in the holder with a new CR2016 lithium battery. Make sure the + and – sides are facing the correct direction before reinserting it.
<PIC>

4. Insert the holder back securely, then close the battery compartment cover.
<PIC>

Important: Use only a CR2016 lithium battery for the date/time back-up battery.,['Manual10_47', 'Manual10_48', 'Manual10_49', 'Manual10_50']

To focus on an off-center subject, use focus lock. This lets you focus on the subject first, lock that focus distance, and then recompose the shot so the subject can remain off-center in your final framing.

Important: Focus lock works only in One-Shot AF mode. It will not work in AI Servo AF, or when AI Focus AF has switched to Servo operation.

Steps to use focus lock for an off-center subject:

1. Set the Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode, such as P, Tv, Av, M, or A-DEP.

2. Select the AF point you want to use.

3. Place the selected AF point over your subject, then press the shutter button halfway to focus.



4. Keep holding the shutter button halfway down. While holding it, recompose the picture so the subject is positioned where you want in the frame.

5. Press the shutter button fully to take the picture.

Note: Focus lock can also be used in Basic Zone modes except Sports mode. In that case, you can start directly from step 3: place the AF point on the subject, press the shutter button halfway to focus, keep holding it, recompose, and shoot.,['Manual10_107']

When using the self-timer or an optional remote switch, your eye may not be covering the viewfinder. In this situation, stray light can enter through the viewfinder eyepiece and may affect the exposure of the photo. To prevent this, attach the eyepiece cover before taking the shot.

Steps to use the eyepiece cover:

1. Remove the eyecup  
Hold the eyecup from the bottom and push it upward to slide it off the viewfinder.

<PIC>

2. Attach the eyepiece cover  
Take the eyepiece cover from the camera strap, align it with the eyepiece groove, and slide it downward until it is securely attached.

<PIC>

Tip: The eyepiece cover is normally attached to the camera strap, so keeping the strap installed makes it easy to use whenever you shoot with the self-timer or remote switch.,['Manual10_154', 'Manual10_155']

To select the AF Mode before taking a picture, please follow these steps:

1. Set the lens to autofocus.
On the lens, move the focus mode switch to <AF>. If the lens is set to <MF>, autofocus mode selection will not work as expected.

<PIC>

2. Set the Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode.
Turn the Mode Dial to one of the Creative Zone modes, such as P, Tv, Av, M, or A-DEP.

Please note: In Basic Zone modes, the camera selects the AF mode automatically, so you cannot manually choose the AF mode.

3. Press the <AF-WB> button.
Press the <AF-WB> button on the top of the camera to activate AF mode selection.

<PIC>

4. Select the AF mode on the LCD panel.
While looking at the LCD panel, turn the Main Dial until the desired AF mode appears.

<PIC>

You can choose from the following AF modes:

- ONE SHOT / One-Shot AF: Best for still subjects. Focus is achieved once when you press the shutter button halfway, and focus remains locked while you keep the button half-pressed.
- AI FOCUS / AI Focus AF: Best when the subject may start moving. The camera starts in One-Shot AF and automatically switches to AI Servo AF if it detects subject movement.
- AI SERVO / AI Servo AF: Best for moving subjects. The camera continuously tracks focus while you press the shutter button halfway.

In short: use One-Shot AF for stationary subjects, AI Servo AF for moving subjects, and AI Focus AF when you want the camera to switch automatically if the subject begins moving.,['Manual10_99', 'Camera_30', 'Camera_31']

To set the camera to “P” mode / Program AE, follow these steps: 1. Turn the Mode Dial to <P>. P stands for Program AE. In this mode, the camera automatically sets the shutter speed and aperture value according to the subject’s brightness, making it a convenient general-purpose shooting mode. 2. Check the exposure display. Press the shutter button halfway, then check the shutter speed and aperture value shown in the viewfinder and on the top LCD panel. These values are set automatically by the camera. A correct exposure can be obtained as long as the shutter speed and aperture value are displayed normally and are not blinking. 3. Take the picture. Compose your shot, focus on the subject, and then press the shutter button completely to take the photo. Note: If the shutter speed and aperture values blink, the exposure may not be correct. For example, blinking values can indicate underexposure or overexposure, so you may need to adjust ISO, use flash, or reduce the amount of light entering the lens.,['Manual10_116', 'Manual10_117', 'Manual10_119']

You can view the camera’s captured images on a TV for a larger and clearer display by connecting the camera to the TV with the provided video cable. Please note that the TV display is used for image playback; once the image appears on the TV, the camera’s LCD monitor will not display the image at the same time.

Before connecting or disconnecting the cable, always turn off both the camera and the TV to avoid connection problems.

1. Open the camera’s terminal cover.
2. Connect the video cable to the camera’s video terminal.
3. Connect the other end of the cable to the TV’s VIDEO IN terminal.
4. Make sure both cable plugs are inserted fully and securely.
5. Turn on the TV, then switch the TV input/source to Video IN.

<PIC>

6. Turn on the camera and press the playback button on the camera.
7. The captured image will appear on the TV screen. At this time, nothing will be shown on the camera’s LCD monitor.

<PIC>

After you finish viewing, turn off the camera and the TV first, then disconnect the video cable.

Note: If the image does not display correctly, or the colors/format look abnormal, check that the camera’s [Video system] setting matches your TV’s video system. Also, depending on the TV model, the edges of the image may be slightly cut off on the screen.,['Manual10_182', 'Manual10_183']

To delete just one photo from your camera, follow these steps:

1. Enter playback mode and display the photo.
Press the playback button to show the images stored on the camera. The most recent photo will appear on the LCD monitor.
<PIC>

2. Choose the image you want to delete.
Turn the dial to browse through your photos until the unwanted image is displayed on the screen.

3. Open the erase menu.
Press the erase button, marked with the trash can icon. The erase options will appear at the bottom of the LCD screen.
<PIC>

4. Confirm deletion of that single image.
Turn the dial to select [Erase], then press SET. The access lamp will blink while the camera deletes the image.
<PIC>

If you want to delete more individual images, simply repeat steps 2–4 for each photo.

Please note: once an image is erased, it cannot be recovered, so make sure you no longer need it before confirming. Also, protected images cannot be deleted until their protection is canceled first.,['Manual10_184', 'Manual10_185', 'Manual10_186']

Yes, you can erase all images on the CF card at once. Please note that protected images will not be erased by this operation.

Important: Once images are erased, they cannot be recovered. Please make sure you no longer need them before continuing. If there are any photos you may want to keep, transfer them to a computer first.

Steps to erase all unprotected images:

1. Enter playback mode  
Press the playback button to display an image on the camera screen.

2. Open the erase menu  
Press the erase button with the trash can icon. The Erase menu will appear at the bottom of the screen.

<PIC>

3. Select [All]  
Turn the dial to highlight [All], then press SET. A confirmation screen will appear.

<PIC>

4. Confirm erasing  
Turn the dial to select [OK], then press SET. The camera will erase all unprotected images on the CF card.

<PIC>

Please note:
- Protected images will remain on the card. To erase them, you need to cancel their protection first.
- If you want to completely clear the CF card, including protected images, you can format the CF card instead. Formatting removes everything on the card, so please back up any needed files before doing it.,['Camera_58', 'Camera_59', 'Manual10_187']

To print photos directly from the camera using the CP Direct method, follow these steps:

1. **Prepare the camera and printer**
   - Make sure the photos you want to print are saved on the camera’s CF card.
   - Use a fully charged camera battery, or use an AC power source if available, because direct printing can take some time.
   - Turn off both the camera and the printer before connecting them.

2. **Connect the camera to a CP Direct-compatible printer**
   - Use the appropriate interface cable for your printer type.
   - Connect the cable to the camera’s **DIGITAL** terminal, making sure the plug is inserted in the correct direction.

If the charging case battery is low, you can recharge it with the USB-C charging cable as follows:

When Bluetooth is pairing and connecting, you may encounter the following scenarios:

1. Earbuds powering on and entering pairing mode
When you open the charging case, the earbuds will first connect to each other, and then they will enter Bluetooth pairing mode so they can connect to your phone or another device.

While the earbuds are still inside the charging case, the earbud LEDs show the charging status instead of the Bluetooth status:
- Solid red: Low battery
- Flashing light blue: Charging
- Solid blue: Fully charged

<PIC>

2. Earbuds removed from the case and showing pairing status
After you take the earbuds out of the case, the LED indicators will show their Bluetooth connection status:
- Flashing light blue: The earbuds are in pairing mode and ready to connect
- Solid light blue: The earbuds are connected successfully

<PIC>

3. Connecting from your phone or device
On your phone, go to the Bluetooth settings page and make sure Bluetooth is turned ON. Then select the available earphones device from the Bluetooth list to connect.

<PIC>

Please note: If the earbuds were previously paired with a nearby device, they may automatically reconnect to that device instead of entering pairing mode for a new device.

4. Automatic reconnection scenario
If your earbuds have already been connected to your phone, tablet, or computer before, they may reconnect automatically when:
- The earbuds are powered on
- Bluetooth is enabled on the previously paired device
- The device is nearby and within Bluetooth range

If you want to pair them with a different device, turn Bluetooth OFF on the previously connected device first. The earbuds should then become available for pairing with the new device.

5. Multipoint Bluetooth connection scenario
These earbuds also support multipoint connection, which allows them to connect to up to two devices, such as a smartphone and a computer.

To set this up:
1. On the device that is already paired, go to Bluetooth settings and turn Bluetooth OFF.
2. The earbuds will disconnect and become available for pairing.
3. Pair the earbuds with your second device.
4. Turn Bluetooth back ON on the first device.
5. The earbuds should automatically reconnect to the first device, so both devices are paired.

After this, you can use either connected device for music or calls.,['Manual12_5', 'Manual12_6', 'Manual12_7']

“Controling” should be spelled “controlling.”

The earphones have two main controlling functions: music control and call control. You can use the multifunction button on either earbud to operate these features.

1. Controlling Music
You can control music playback directly from the earbuds, including:
- Play/Pause: press either the left or right earbud once.
- Volume Up: press the left earbud 2 times.
- Volume Down: press the right earbud 2 times.
- Previous Track: press the left earbud 3 times.
- Next Track: press the right earbud 3 times.

<PIC>

2. Controlling Calls
You can also manage phone calls using the earbuds, including:
- Answer a Call: press either the left or right earbud.
- End a Call: press and hold either the left or right earbud.
- Reject a Call: press and hold either the left or right earbud when a call is coming in.

<PIC>,['Manual12_8', 'Manual12_9']

Besides the two main controls for music playback and calls, you can also operate the following earphone functions using the multifunction buttons:

1. Voice Assistant
Press and hold the left earbud until you hear the first beep, then release. This will activate your phone’s voice assistant.

<PIC>

2. Music App
Press and hold the right earbud until you hear the first beep, then release to activate the music app. The app will resume playback from where you left off. If there was no previous session, or if you want to start other music, tap again and the music app will play recommended songs.

<PIC>

3. Ambient Awareness and ANC Modes
Press the left earbud to switch Ambient Awareness and ANC modes on or off. Each press cycles through the available listening modes, so you can choose the setting that best matches your environment.

<PIC>

4. Low Latency Mode
Low Latency Mode helps reduce Bluetooth audio delay, which is especially useful for gaming and keeping audio in sync with video. To turn Low Latency Mode on or off, press and hold either earbud until you hear the second beep, then release.

<PIC>,['earphones_01', 'earphones_02', 'earphones_03', 'Manual12_10']

If your earphones are not working properly, you can reset them using the buttons on both earbuds. Before resetting, please make sure the earbuds are disconnected from Bluetooth and removed from the charging case.

1. First, disconnect the earbuds from Bluetooth on your phone or device. If needed, you can also forget/remove the earbuds from the Bluetooth device list.
2. Take both earbuds out of the charging case.
3. Press and hold the buttons on both earbuds at the same time.

For a factory reset, press and hold both earbuds for 6 seconds. This will erase all settings and pairing information, so you may need to pair the earbuds again afterward.

<PIC>

If the earbuds are frozen, locked up, or completely unresponsive, you can perform a hardware reset instead. Press and hold both earbuds for 10 seconds to restart them. After the reset, press the earbuds again to power them back on.

<PIC>

Note: A hardware reset is only needed in rare cases when the earbuds are unresponsive. For most connection or pairing issues, the 6-second factory reset is usually the best option.,['Manual12_11', 'Manual12_12']

Here are the recommended maintenance and care policies for your Active Noise Canceling Truly Wireless Earphones:

<PIC>

- **Clean gently and keep them dry:** Use a soft, clean cloth or paper towel to wipe the earbuds and charging case. Do not use harsh chemicals, detergents, sprays, or abrasive cleaners. Always make sure the earphones and charging contacts are completely dry before placing them in the charging case or charging them.

- **Store properly when not in use:** Keep the earphones in a cool, dry place, preferably inside the charging case. Avoid leaving them in direct sunlight, humid areas, or places where they may be exposed to water.

- **Handle the charging cable carefully:** When connecting or disconnecting the USB-C charging cable from the charging case, do so gently. Never pull, tug, or yank the cable while it is connected, as this may damage the cable or charging port.

- **Avoid extreme temperatures:** Do not expose the earphones or charging case to excessive heat, fire, direct sunlight, or extreme cold. Avoid temperatures above 100°F, and do not leave the product in places such as a hot car or near heaters.

- **Protect from impact and excessive force:** Do not drop, crush, or apply strong pressure to the earbuds or charging case. Physical damage caused by impact or misuse may affect performance and charging.

- **Recycle or dispose of responsibly:** The earphones contain a lithium polymer battery, so they should be recycled or disposed of according to your local regulations. Please contact your local recycling facility or the manufacturer for proper disposal guidance.,['Manual12_13']

The eReader’s **Main Menu** and **Browser History** help you quickly access the device’s main functions and return to recently opened books.

**1. Main Menu**

The **Main Menu** is the central screen where you can access all major functions of the device. It includes the following options:

- **Browser History**: View recently opened files
- **eBook**: Open and read supported eBook files
- **Music**: Play audio files
- **Video**: Play video files
- **Photo**: View pictures
- **Record**: Use voice recording functions
- **Explorer**: Browse files stored on the device or Micro SD card
- **Settings**: Adjust device settings

Use the **Navigation keys** to move between options, then press the **“M” button** to enter the selected function. You can press the **Home/ESC** button to return to the previous screen.

<PIC>

**2. Browser History**

The **Browser History** function displays files that you have recently opened, especially recently read books. This makes it easier to continue reading without manually searching for the file again.

To use it:

1. Enter **Browser History** from the Main Menu.
2. Use the **Navigation keys** to select the book or file you want to open.
3. Press the **“M” button** to enter.
4. The device will open the selected book and return to the **last page you read**.

<PIC>

**Important note:** If a recently opened file was stored on a **Micro SD card** and that file has been deleted or the card is removed, the record may still appear in the **Browser History** list. However, the file cannot be opened unless it is available again in its original storage location.,['Manual13_3', 'Manual13_4']

Here are the common troubles you may encounter when using this eReader, along with the recommended solutions. You can also refer to the button layout below to locate the Power button, USB port, Micro SD card slot, and Reset On/Off switch more easily.

<PIC>

1. The eReader cannot power on

Possible causes and solutions:
- The battery may be too low. Please connect the eReader with the USB cable and charge it until the battery indicator bar is full, then try turning it on again.
- Make sure you press and hold the Power button for about 3–5 seconds.
- If the device still cannot power on after a full charge, the device may be defective. Please contact the manufacturer or seller for further assistance.

2. Files cannot be downloaded or transferred to the eReader

Possible causes and solutions:
- The USB cable may be faulty or may only support charging. Please try another USB data cable.
- The eReader’s memory may be full. Please delete unnecessary files or transfer some files to a Micro SD card.
- After connecting to a computer, the eReader should appear as a removable disk. If a Micro SD card is inserted, the computer may show two removable disks: one for the built-in memory and one for the Micro SD card.
- If you are using a very old Windows system such as Windows 98, a USB 2.0 driver may be required for the computer to recognize the eReader.

3. Music files cannot be played

Possible causes and solutions:
- The eReader supports many common audio formats, but some newer music formats, special codecs, or high-resolution audio files may not be compatible.
- Please try converting the music file to a more common format, then transfer it to the eReader again.
- If the issue continues with multiple music files, please contact technical support for help.

4. The Micro SD card cannot be recognized

Recommended steps:
- Turn off the eReader first.
- Remove the Micro SD card.
- Reinsert the card securely.
- Turn the eReader back on and check whether the card is detected.

Important notes for Micro SD card use:
- Always power off the device before inserting or removing the Micro SD card.
- Do not reset the eReader while a Micro SD card is inserted.
- The device supports Micro SD/SDHC cards up to 16GB.

5. The device freezes or has a minor operating problem

If the eReader becomes unresponsive or has a minor issue:
- You may use the Reset On/Off switch located on the back of the device near the card reader.
- Before resetting, remove the Micro SD card to avoid possible file or card issues.
- After resetting, restart the eReader and check whether it works normally.

6. General care and safety tips

To help avoid problems during daily use:
- Do not drop, crush, bend, or apply strong pressure to the device.
- Keep the screen protected when not in use to avoid scratches or damage.
- Do not expose the eReader to moisture, rain, extreme temperatures, dust, or harmful conditions.
- Clean it only with a soft, dry cloth. Do not use harsh chemicals.
- If the device gets wet, overheats, is damaged from a fall, or still does not work after correct operation, please do not disassemble it yourself. Contact the manufacturer or seller for assistance.,['Manual13_0']

To properly finish connecting the fax, please complete the connection in this order and keep the following safety points in mind:

1. **Place the machine in a safe location**
   - Put the product on a flat, stable surface.
   - Position it near both an easily accessible AC power outlet and a telephone wall jack.
   - Keep cords arranged so they do not create a tripping hazard, and do not block access to the power outlet.

2. **Connect the telephone line correctly**
   - Use only a **No. 26 AWG or larger telecommunication line cord**.
   - Connect the fax to the telephone line using a standard modular telephone jack, **USOC RJ11C**.
   - Do not install or modify telephone wiring during a thunderstorm, and do not install a telephone wall jack in a wet or damp location.

3. **Connect the power safely**
   - If a power cord was provided with the product, use **only that provided power cord**.
   - Make sure the local power supply matches the rating shown on the product’s rating label.
   - Plug the grounded plug into a properly grounded AC power outlet.

<PIC>

4. **Final safety checks before use**
   - Do not connect the product to a DC power source or inverter.
   - Do not use an extension cord if possible.
   - Make sure the power cord is not stretched, pinched, frayed, or placed where people may step on it.
   - Keep the outlet accessible at all times. In an emergency, unplug the power cord from the AC outlet to shut off power completely.

5. **If you need to service, move, or change the connection later**
   - First unplug the telephone line cord from the wall jack.
   - Then unplug the power cord from the AC outlet.
   - Disconnect any other connected cables before installing, servicing, or modifying the equipment.

If the product becomes unusually hot, produces smoke, gives off a strong smell, or liquid/metal objects get inside it, unplug it from the AC power outlet immediately and contact authorized service support.,['Manual15_2']

To help ensure your safety when using this fax, please pay attention to the following precautions:

1. Follow all safety warnings on the product
Read and follow all warnings and labels marked on the fax. Ignoring them may increase the risk of personal injury or property damage, including fire, electric shock, burns, or suffocation.

2. Place the fax in a safe location
Put the fax on a flat, level, stable surface, such as a desk, where it is free from vibration and shocks. Place it near a telephone wall jack and an easily accessible standard AC power outlet.

Please also note:
- Do not place the fax on an unstable cart, stand, or table, especially where children may be nearby.
- Keep it out of busy areas where it could be accidentally knocked over.
- Secure all cables and cords so they do not create a tripping hazard.
- Do not place heavy objects on the fax.
- Do not block access to the power outlet, so the fax can be unplugged quickly in an emergency.
- Do not place anything in front of the fax that may block printed pages or received faxes.

3. Prevent electrical hazards
Do not use the fax during a thunderstorm, as there may be a remote risk of electric shock from lightning. If the fax has been dropped or the casing is damaged, unplug it from the AC power outlet immediately and contact authorized service personnel.

Also, do not dismantle or attempt to repair the fax yourself. Opening or removing covers may expose you to dangerous voltage points or other risks.

<PIC>

4. Prevent fire hazards when cleaning
Do not use flammable substances, sprays, alcohol, ammonia, organic solvents, or other liquids to clean the inside or outside of the fax. These may cause a fire. Use only a dry, lint-free cloth for cleaning.

<PIC>

5. Use the power cord safely
Use only the power cord provided with the fax, if one was included. Make sure the fax is connected to the correct AC power source indicated on the product rating label.

For safe power cord use:
- Do not place anything on the power cord.
- Do not place the fax where people may step on the cord.
- Do not stretch, strain, pinch, or constrict the cord.
- Do not use the fax if the power cord is frayed or damaged.
- Do not pull the cord from the middle; hold the plug when unplugging.
- Do not use extension cords.
- Do not use undesignated or incompatible cables.

6. Be careful with telephone lines
When installing or modifying telephone lines, never touch uninsulated telephone wires or terminals unless the telephone line has been unplugged from the wall jack.

For fax models, please also follow these precautions:
- Never install telephone wiring during a thunderstorm.
- Never install a telephone wall jack in a wet or potentially wet location.
- Do not use the fax near water or in damp areas, such as near a bathtub, sink, washing machine, wet basement, or swimming pool.
- Do not use the fax to report a gas leak while near the leak.
- Use only a No. 26 AWG or larger telecommunication line cord.

7. Avoid injury from moving parts, trays, and paper handling
Wait until pages have completely exited the fax before picking them up. Otherwise, your fingers may be caught inside the machine.

Please also be careful to:
- Keep your hands and foreign objects out of the ink insertion slot.
- Avoid placing the fax so that an extended paper tray protrudes beyond the edge of the desk or table. If someone hits the tray, the fax could fall and cause injury.
- Keep your hands away from moving parts while the fax is operating.

The image below shows the correct placement when the paper tray is extended. The tray should not stick out beyond the table edge.

<PIC>

To prevent finger injuries, do not place your fingers in the areas shown below, especially around covers, hinges, and other pinch points.

<PIC>

8. Stop using the fax immediately if something abnormal happens
If the fax becomes unusually hot, releases smoke, produces a strong smell, or if liquid is spilled on it, immediately unplug it from the AC power outlet and contact authorized service personnel.

You should also unplug the fax and request service if:
- Liquid has entered the product.
- The fax has been exposed to rain or water.
- The fax does not operate normally even when used correctly.
- There is a noticeable change in performance.

9. Keep packaging materials away from children
Plastic bags used for packaging are not toys. Keep them away from babies and children to avoid the risk of suffocation, and dispose of them properly.

10. Additional safety notes
- Do not place metal objects, such as clips, staples, or jewelry, on the fax. If they contact electrical parts inside the product, they may cause fire or electric shock.
- For wireless models, do not use the fax near medical electrical equipment. If you use a pacemaker and feel anything unusual near the fax, move away from it and consult a doctor.
- Keep ink cartridges out of reach of children. If ink contacts your skin, wash with soap and water. If ink gets into your eyes or mouth, rinse immediately and seek medical advice.

To connect the regulator to the LP tank safely, please follow these steps:

1. **Make sure the LP tank is properly secured on the grill.**  
   The tank should be upright and firmly installed in the correct position before connecting the regulator.

2. **Turn all grill control knobs to the OFF position.**  
   This helps prevent gas from flowing through the burners during connection.

3. **Close the LP tank valve completely.**  
   Turn the OPD hand wheel clockwise until it stops.

4. **Remove the protective cap from the LP tank valve.**  
   Always keep and use the cap and strap supplied with the valve when the tank is not connected.

<PIC>

**Important:** Do not use a POL transport plug, which is the plastic plug with external threads. Using it can defeat the safety function of the valve.

<PIC>

5. **Align the regulator with the LP tank valve.**  
   Hold the regulator straight and insert the regulator nipple into the LP tank valve. The nipple must be centered in the valve opening to avoid cross-threading.

<PIC>

6. **Hand-tighten the coupling nut.**  
   Turn the coupling nut clockwise until it reaches a full stop. You may feel some resistance as the regulator seals against the back-check feature inside the LP tank valve. After resistance is felt, turn it an additional **1/2 to 3/4 turn** to complete the connection.

**Tighten by hand only. Do not use tools.**

If you cannot complete the connection, disconnect the regulator and repeat steps 5 and 6. If it still cannot be connected properly, do not use that regulator.

Safety reminders:
- Do not insert tools or foreign objects into the valve outlet or safety relief valve.
- If you detect a gas leak at any time, stop immediately and call the fire department or your LP gas supplier.
- Do not use the grill until the connection has been leak-tested.
- If you smell gas, shut off gas to the appliance, extinguish any open flame, open the lid, and move away if the odor continues.,['Manual19_16', 'Manual19_17', 'Manual19_18']

You likely mean: “To ensure my safety when using the grill, how do I do leak testing on the valves, hose, and regulator?”

Here is the safe way to perform the leak test:

1. Turn all grill control knobs to OFF.

2. Make sure the regulator is tightly connected to the LP tank. The coupling nut should be hand-tightened securely—do not use tools.

3. Fully open the LP tank valve by turning the OPD hand wheel counterclockwise.
   - If you hear a rushing sound, turn the gas off immediately.
   - This indicates a major leak at the connection.
   - Do not continue until the problem is corrected. For replacement parts, call Sears at 1-800-4-MY-HOME.

4. Prepare a leak-test solution using mild soap and water, then brush the solution onto the connection points shown below. Focus on the LP tank valve area, regulator connection, hose connections, and manifold/valve joints where bubbles are indicated.

<PIC>

5. Watch carefully for “growing” bubbles.
   - If bubbles grow or continue forming, there is a gas leak.
   - Immediately close the LP tank valve by turning the hand wheel clockwise.
   - Retighten the connections and repeat the leak test.
   - If the leak cannot be stopped, do not try to repair it yourself. Call Sears at 1-800-4-MY-HOME for replacement parts.

6. After completing the leak test, always close the LP tank valve by turning the hand wheel clockwise.

<PIC>

Important safety reminders:

- Do not use the grill until it has been leak-tested.
- Do not use an open flame to check for gas leaks.
- Perform the leak test outdoors in a well-ventilated area, away from flames, sparks, electrical appliances, or other ignition sources.
- Do not smoke during the leak test.
- If a leak is detected at any time and cannot be stopped, immediately close the LP tank valve and call your LP gas supplier or the fire department.
- If you smell gas: shut off the gas, extinguish any open flame, open the lid, and if the odor continues, keep away from the grill and call your gas supplier or fire department immediately.,['Manual19_20', 'Manual19_21']

Sure — for indirect cooking using the grill, please pay attention to the following points:

First, the word should be “about” rather than “ablout.”

Indirect cooking is especially suitable for poultry and larger cuts of meat. Instead of placing food directly over the flame, you turn on selected burners and leave the burner area under the food off. This allows heat to circulate gently inside the grill, cooking the food slowly and evenly without direct flame contact.

This method is helpful when cooking fattier foods because it greatly reduces flare-ups: dripping fat and juices are less likely to ignite when there is no direct flame directly underneath the food.

<PIC>

For the best results when using indirect cooking:

1. Keep the lid closed while cooking.  
   This helps maintain steady heat circulation inside the grill and allows the food to roast more evenly.

2. Set the burners correctly.  
   Place the food over an area where the burner is OFF, while the surrounding burner or burners provide heat. For example, on a multi-burner grill, you may use the outside burners and keep the center burners off for indirect heat.

<PIC>

3. Adjust cooking time and temperature based on weather.  
   Outdoor conditions can affect grill temperature. In cold or windy weather, cooking may take longer, and you may need to increase the temperature setting to maintain sufficient cooking heat.

4. Monitor the grill safely.  
   Do not leave the grill unattended while preheating, cooking, or burning off food residue on high heat.

5. Be prepared for grease flare-ups or grease fires.  
   If a grease fire develops, turn all control knobs to OFF and turn off the LP tank valve. Do not use water on a grease fire, as this may cause injury or make the situation worse.

In short, indirect cooking works best with the lid closed, the food placed away from direct flame, and the burners arranged to create gentle circulating heat. It is ideal for slow roasting, larger cuts, poultry, and foods that need more even cooking with fewer flare-ups.,['Manual19_36', 'Manual19_37']

Absolutely — here are the key safety tips to follow when using your grill:

<PIC>

### General safe-use guidelines
- **Outdoor use only:** Use the grill only in a well-ventilated outdoor area. Never use it in an enclosed or covered space such as a garage, carport, porch, covered patio, or under any overhead structure.
- **Keep proper clearance:** Place the grill at least **3 ft. away from any wall or surface**, and keep at least **10 ft. away from anything that can catch fire** or ignition sources such as pilot lights, electrical appliances, etc.
- **Keep children away:** Do not allow children to operate the grill or play near it.
- **Keep the area clear:** Make sure the grill area is free of flammable materials, gasoline, or other flammable liquids/vapors.
- **Do not move while hot:** Never move the grill while it is operating or still hot.
- **Use proper tools:** Use long-handled barbecue utensils and oven mitts to help prevent burns and splatters.
- **Grease tray safety:** The grease tray must be installed during use and should be emptied after each use. Wait until the grill has completely cooled before removing it.
- **Do not leave unattended:** Never leave the grill unattended while preheating or burning off food residue on high.
- **Turn off after use:** When the grill is not in use, turn all control knobs to OFF and close the LP tank valve.
- **Before opening the LP tank valve:** Check that the coupling nut is tight.

<PIC>

### If you smell gas
If you smell gas at any time:
1. Shut off gas to the appliance.
2. Extinguish any open flame.
3. Open the lid.
4. If the odor continues, stay away from the grill and immediately call your gas supplier or fire department.

### Extra precautions while lighting
- Always open the lid before lighting the grill.
- Do not lean over the grill while lighting.
- If the burner does not ignite quickly, turn the control knobs to OFF and close the valve.

<PIC>

### If the burner does not light
If the burner does not light, turn the knobs to **OFF**, wait **5 minutes**, and then try again. Always close the valve during this 5-minute waiting period so gas can safely clear before relighting.,['Manual19_23', 'Manual19_24', 'Manual19_25']

Your WaveRunner has **three important identification numbers**. These numbers are used to identify the watercraft and engine, especially when ordering spare parts from a jetski dealer or if you ever need to report the watercraft as stolen. It’s a good idea to write all three down and keep a separate copy in a safe place.

### 1. Primary Identification Number — **PRI-ID**
The **PRI-ID number** identifies the watercraft model/primary unit. It is shown on a label with the model and PRI-ID format.

<PIC>

You can find this PRI-ID label **inside the engine compartment**. Look for the label marked by the circled **1** in the image below.

<PIC>

### 2. Hull Identification Number — **HIN**
The **HIN** is the identification number for the hull. It normally starts with a manufacturer/country code format, as shown below.

<PIC>

You can find the HIN on a plate attached to the hull at the **rear right side** of the watercraft — this is called the **aft, starboard side**. “Starboard” means the right side when you are facing forward on the watercraft.

<PIC>

### 3. Engine Serial Number
The **engine serial number** identifies the engine itself. It is printed/stamped on an engine identification label.

<PIC>

You can find this label **on the engine unit** inside the engine compartment. In the image below, the circled **1** points to the location of the engine serial number label.

<PIC>

### Quick location summary
- **PRI-ID:** Inside the engine compartment
- **HIN:** Rear right side of the hull
- **Engine serial number:** On the engine unit

So, when someone asks for your jetski’s identification numbers, they usually mean these three: **PRI-ID, HIN, and engine serial number**.,['Manual20_0', 'jetski_01', 'Manual20_1', 'jetski_02', 'Manual20_2', 'jetski_03']

Your jetski does have several important cruising limitations and safe-operating restrictions. Please keep the following points in mind whenever you ride:

<PIC>

1. Keep a constant lookout
Always scan the water around you for swimmers, floating objects, shoreline hazards, and other boats or watercraft. Pay extra attention in situations where your view may be blocked or visibility is reduced.

2. Maintain safe speed and distance
Operate defensively at a safe speed, and keep a safe distance from people, objects, other watercraft, and boats. Do not follow directly behind another boat or watercraft, as this increases collision risk and may expose you to wakes or sudden maneuvers.

3. Do not spray or splash others
Do not ride close to people, boats, or other riders in order to spray or splash them. This can be dangerous and may prevent others from predicting your movement.

4. Avoid unpredictable or aggressive maneuvers
Avoid sharp turns, sudden direction changes, or other maneuvers that make it difficult for others to avoid you or understand where you are going. Ride within your skill level and avoid aggressive operation to reduce the risk of losing control, being ejected, or colliding with another object or vessel.

5. Stay away from shallow or obstructed areas
Avoid areas where submerged objects may be present, and do not ride in shallow water. Hitting an underwater object can cause damage, loss of control, or injury.

6. Do not operate in rough weather or poor visibility
Do not operate the watercraft in rough water, bad weather, fog, heavy rain, or any condition where visibility is poor. Before setting out, check the weather forecast and pay attention to current conditions, since adverse weather can quickly increase the risk of an accident.

7. Avoid riding alone far from shore
As with any water sport, it is best not to ride without someone nearby. If you ride farther from shore than you could safely swim, ride with another boat or watercraft nearby while still maintaining a safe distance.

<PIC>

8. Minimum water depth: 60 cm / 2 ft
Never operate the watercraft in water less than 60 cm, or 2 ft, deep. Operating in shallower water increases the chance of striking submerged objects, which could result in injury or damage.

9. Do not jump wakes or waves
This is a high-performance watercraft, not a toy. Sharp turns or jumping wakes and waves can increase the risk of serious injuries, including back or spinal injury, facial injury, broken legs, broken ankles, or other broken bones. For safety, do not jump wakes or waves.

<PIC>

10. No night operation
This watercraft is not equipped with the lighting required for night operation. Do not operate it after sunset or before dawn. Riding in darkness greatly increases the risk of colliding with another boat or object, which could result in severe injury or death.,['Manual20_16', 'Manual20_17', 'Manual20_18']

To operate your jetski safely, please follow these key operation requirements before and during every ride:

1. Wear the required protective gear

All riders must wear a Coast Guard-approved personal flotation device (PFD) suitable for personal watercraft use.

You should also wear protective lower-body clothing. If a rider falls into the water or is near the jet thrust nozzle, water can be forced into body cavities and cause severe internal injuries. Normal swimwear does not provide enough protection. All riders must wear a wetsuit bottom or equivalent protective clothing, such as thick, tightly woven, sturdy, snug-fitting apparel like denim.

<PIC>

In the image above:
1. Coast Guard-approved PFD
2. Wetsuit bottom or equivalent protective clothing

Eye protection is recommended to protect against wind, water spray, and sun glare. Footwear and gloves are also recommended for better protection and grip.

2. Consider helmet use carefully

Helmets meeting Snell or DOT standards are required for IJSBA-sanctioned races. For recreational riding, decide based on your riding conditions, environment, riding style, traffic level, and water surface conditions.

A helmet may help protect your head in certain collisions, but it can also create risks in the water, such as catching water during a fall, reducing vision or hearing, or increasing fatigue. If you choose to wear one, select a helmet suitable for personal watercraft use whenever possible.

3. Never ride under the influence

Never operate the watercraft after consuming alcohol or taking drugs. Reaction time, judgment, balance, and control can all be seriously affected.

<PIC>

4. Complete checks before riding

For both safety and proper care of the watercraft, always perform preoperation checks before you start riding. Make sure the craft is in proper condition and that required safety equipment is on board.

5. Keep proper riding posture

When the watercraft is moving, the operator and passenger should always keep both feet in the footwell. Lifting your feet increases the chance of losing balance or being struck by objects outside the craft.

Do not carry a child passenger if the child’s feet cannot reach the floor of the footwell.

<PIC>

The passenger should sit securely behind the operator and firmly hold on to the operator at all times.

6. Check health conditions before riding

If you are pregnant or in poor health, consult your doctor before riding to confirm whether it is safe for you.

7. Do not modify the watercraft

Do not attempt to modify the watercraft. Modifications may reduce safety and reliability, and may make the watercraft unsafe or illegal to use.

8. Ride defensively and watch your surroundings

Scan carefully for swimmers, boats, objects, ski ropes, fishing lines, and other hazards. Stay away from swimming areas, as swimmers can be difficult to see.

Maintain a safe distance from other boats and watercraft, obey the applicable navigation rules, and always check behind you before making a turn.

<PIC>

In short: wear the correct safety gear, never ride under the influence, check the watercraft before use, keep all riders properly seated with feet in the footwells, avoid unsafe modifications, and maintain constant awareness of people, boats, and hazards around you.,['Manual20_19', 'Manual20_20', 'Manual20_21', 'Manual20_22']

Your jetski has several important watercraft handling and propulsion characteristics that affect how it steers, moves, and should be operated safely.

First, the watercraft turns by jet thrust. When you turn the handlebars, the jet thrust nozzle changes direction, and the watercraft turns according to the direction of that thrust. Because of this, steering depends not only on handlebar position, but also on throttle input.

If you release the throttle completely, the watercraft produces only minimum thrust. At speeds above trolling speed, your ability to steer will decrease quickly without throttle. You may still have a little turning response immediately after releasing the throttle, but once the engine slows down, the watercraft will no longer respond effectively to handlebar input until you apply throttle again or slow to trolling speed.

For this reason, practice turning in an open area away from people, boats, and obstacles until you are comfortable with how the watercraft responds. If debris or weeds collect around the jet intake, stop the engine and remove the clip from the engine shut-off switch before clearing anything.

<PIC>

Second, this watercraft is water-jet propelled. The jet pump is directly connected to the engine, so whenever the engine is running, jet thrust can create some movement. There is no true “neutral” position.

For safety, keep away from the intake grate while the engine is on. Long hair, loose clothing, or PFD straps can become caught in moving parts and may cause severe injury or drowning. Also, never insert any object into the jet thrust nozzle while the engine is running, because contact with rotating jet pump parts can result in severe injury or death.

<PIC>,['Manual20_24', 'Manual20_25']

Your jetski is legally considered a powerboat, so when you encounter other vessels you must follow the navigation rules that apply on the waterway where you are riding. In general, operate defensively, keep a proper lookout, maintain a safe speed, and take early action to avoid any risk of collision.

The basic right-of-way terms are:

Stand-on vessel: This is the vessel that has the right-of-way. It should normally maintain its course and speed so the other vessel can predict its movement. However, it must still take action if needed to avoid an immediate collision.

Give-way vessel: This is the vessel that must keep out of the way. If you are the give-way vessel, take clear, early, and timely action. Normally, do not cross in front of the stand-on vessel; slow down or change direction briefly so you can pass safely behind it.

Important: If a collision appears unavoidable, neither vessel has the right-of-way. Both operators must take action to avoid the collision.

<PIC>

The illustration shows the main right-of-way zones around your watercraft. Vessels in the white area generally have priority over you, so you are the give-way vessel. Vessels in the shaded area generally must yield to you. A vessel directly ahead is a meeting situation, where both vessels should alter course to avoid each other.

Meeting head-on: If you are approaching another power-driven vessel head-on and are close enough that a collision could occur, neither vessel has the right-of-way. Both operators should alter course. You should turn so the other vessel passes on your port, or left, side.

<PIC>

Crossing: When two power-driven vessels are crossing paths and there is a risk of collision, the vessel that has the other vessel on its starboard, or right, side must keep out of the way. If the other vessel is on your right side, you are the give-way vessel and should slow, turn, or otherwise maneuver to pass safely behind it. If the other vessel is on your port, or left, side, you are normally the stand-on vessel and should maintain your course and speed, as long as the other vessel is giving way properly.

<PIC>

Overtaking: If you are passing another vessel, you are always the give-way vessel. Stay well clear until you have fully passed and are safely ahead. If another vessel is overtaking you, maintain your speed and direction so the other operator can steer around you safely.

Also follow these special rules and precautions:

1. Narrow channels and bends: Keep to the right when it is safe and practical. When approaching a bend that may block your view of oncoming traffic, sound one prolonged whistle blast of about four to six seconds and proceed carefully.

2. Fishing vessels: Vessels fishing with nets, lines, or trawls generally have the right-of-way regardless of position. Give them plenty of room, and watch for fishing gear in the water.

3. Sailing vessels: Sailing vessels should normally be given the right-of-way. Exceptions include when the sailing vessel is overtaking a power-driven vessel, when it must keep clear of fishing vessels, or when it should not interfere with safe passage in a narrow channel.

For safe operation, constantly scan for other boats, swimmers, objects, ski ropes, and fishing lines. Do not follow directly behind other boats or watercraft, avoid sharp or unpredictable maneuvers, and always check behind you before turning. Remember that a jetski needs throttle to steer effectively, so take early, controlled action instead of waiting until the last moment.,['Manual20_26', 'Manual20_27', 'Manual20_28']

To operate the two engine-related switches on the jetski, use them as follows:

1. Engine stop switch

The engine stop switch is the red button on the handlebar control. Press this switch to stop the engine during normal operation.

<PIC>

2. Engine shut-off switch

The engine shut-off switch is the black button used together with the clip and the engine shut-off cord. Before starting the engine, attach the engine shut-off cord to your wrist, then insert the clip at the end of the cord under the engine shut-off switch. This allows the engine to start and run.

If the clip is pulled out, the engine will stop automatically. This is intended to stop the engine if the operator falls off the watercraft.

<PIC>

Important safety points:

- Always attach the engine shut-off cord to your wrist before starting the engine.
- Always install the clip on the engine shut-off switch before starting.
- When the engine is not running, remove the clip from the shut-off switch to help prevent accidental starting or unauthorized use.
- The engine will not start if the clip is removed from the engine shut-off switch.

<PIC>,['Manual20_43', 'Manual20_44', 'Manual20_45']

There are two main levers you will use on the jetski: the choke lever and the throttle lever. They have different functions, so operate each one as follows:

1. Choke lever

The choke lever is used when starting a cold engine. It helps provide a richer air-fuel mixture, which makes cold starting easier.

<PIC>

- Move lever 1 in direction a to turn the choke on.
- Move lever 1 in direction b to turn the choke off.

Use the choke only as needed for cold starting. After the engine starts and warms up, turn the choke off so the engine can run normally.

2. Throttle lever

The throttle lever controls engine speed and acceleration.

<PIC>

- Squeeze throttle lever 1 to increase engine speed and accelerate.
- Release the throttle lever to reduce engine speed or return the engine to idle.

Important steering note: this jetski needs throttle to steer effectively. The handlebars change the direction of the jet thrust, but the amount of thrust depends on engine speed. If you release the throttle completely while riding faster than trolling speed, steering ability will decrease quickly. For controlled turning, keep some throttle applied unless you are moving at trolling speed.,['Manual20_46', 'Manual20_47']

The Quick Shift Trim System (QSTS) selector is the trim control for your jetski. It is located on the left handlebar grip and lets you adjust the trim angle of the watercraft by changing the vertical angle of the jet thrust nozzle.

<PIC>

The QSTS has 5 selectable positions:

1. Two bow-down positions
2. Neutral “N”
3. Two bow-up positions

The neutral “N” position is suitable for most normal riding conditions. Use the bow-down or bow-up positions when you want to change how the watercraft handles or performs.

<PIC>

To change the trim angle safely:

1. Reduce engine speed to 3,000 r/min or less.
2. Squeeze the shift lock lever.
3. Turn the QSTS selector to the desired position.
